pages.raw_text:
Before going on record on the
question of the park bonds the
Hays Park Improvement club last
night appointed a committee to see
the park board and inquire into
the matter. A resolution was
adopted in favor of terminal rates
in the pending railway franchises.


A building permit for a three.
story brick apartment house on
Third avenue, between Browne and
Mcclellan, has been issued to W.
S. Scott. The cost is 100000.


T. L. Greenouoh, w. J. c. Wake
field and d. w. Twohy have incor.
porated the Greenough Investment
company for 100000.


With a capitliztion of 100000
the Spokane Asphalt Macadam
Paving company has been incor.
porated by F. P. Hogan, Jay P
Graves. Otto Weile and others. 1t
will manufacture and lay the as
phalt macadam that so faVorably
impressed the incorporators or
their recent visit to California.


A question of the legality of the
issue of 100000 of water improve
ment bonds has been raised by the
attorney for the eastern company
that is TO take the bonds. 1t is
claimed that the bonds have ex
ceeded the legal limit that may be
issued without an election some
$4,000, while the city attorney said
that the limit had not been reached
by $4,000 The bond company
wants an election.


A new schedule of freight rates
on Lake Occur d'Alene and the
Occur dAlene river, to take effect
April %, has been announced by
the Inland Empire system, in con
junction with the Perry-Lyon Navi
cation company. The new rates
will make cut into the o. r. & \
territory in the Occur d'Alenes.


Getting into the city hall location
scrap early, the North Side I'M
provement club last night went on
record as favoring the building OF
the new structure in Keystone adj
dition, on Gardner avenue, between
Wall and Washington. There are
two whole blocks that can be had
there.
